sections.paragraph:
Ῥόδη, ης, ἡ (in myths and comedy [Menand., Fgm. 210, 6; 592, 5 Kö.; Philemon Com., Fgm. 84]; Longus 4, 36, 3; 4, 37, 2; Sb 392 [III b.c.]; ins) Rhoda

① a slave in the house of Mary the mother of John Mark Ac 12:13 (Dalman, Arbeit I 365; New Docs 2, 88 poses possibility of assignment as doorkeeper [?]).

② Hermas’ owner Hv 1, 1, 1.—M-M.

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Philemon Com Philemon Com , IV–III b.c. (a few of the verses included here belong to Comp., q.v.)—List 5

Longus Longus , II ?a.d.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

Hermas Hermas , s. H—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 906
